description: Maps each proposal ablation row to the on-disk outputs produced by scripts/run_all_ablations.py. Populated by the orchestrator; read by evaluate_ablations.py and downstream notebooks.

notes.no_external_apis: All 8 configurations run on local Qwen via vLLM. No OpenRouter, no OpenAI, no Groq.
notes.gpt4_substitution: Row 'direct_judge' is labelled 'GPT-4 Direct Judge' in the proposal. Since no external API is used, it is implemented as a strong-judge pass using the same local Qwen on zero-shot pairwise stage-2 relations (no graph). This isolates the targeted-attacks contribution (RQ3: targeted_attacks - direct_judge) and partially overlaps with the graph contribution.
notes.persuasiveness_column: Per-config 'pers_mean_5' in the output is the model's self-reported confidence rescaled to 0-5 Likert. It is a machine-persuasiveness proxy, NOT the human Likert rating described in the proposal. Human ratings require separate annotation of the 50-topic test set.
notes.ablation_design_change_v2_1: schema_version 2.1 fixed a design collapse where direct_judge==targeted_attacks (both shared stage 2) and six_agents==full (both used graph). Now: direct_judge runs stage 2 with zero-shot pairwise attack labelling, targeted_attacks runs stage 2 with Ozaki targeted prompts. six_agents runs WITHOUT graph; full runs WITH graph. This cleanly isolates each ablation variable.
notes.missing_outputs_behavior: If a config's stage4_judgments.json is missing on disk, evaluate_ablations.py marks that row 'missing' in the ablation table and the orchestrator can be re-run to produce it.

ablations.name: direct_judge
ablations.proposal_label: + GPT-4 Direct Judge (local Qwen, zero-shot stage 2, no graph)
ablations.reuses: Stage 1 of 'full' run. Stage 2 runs independently with zero-shot pairwise Attack/Support/None labeller.
ablations.change_note: In schema v2.0 this config shared stage 2 with targeted_attacks, producing identical outputs. Fixed in v2.1 by giving it its own stage 2 path with MAJ_STAGE2_TARGETED=0.

ablations.name: six_agents
ablations.change_note: In schema v2.0 this config used the graph and was identical to full. Fixed in v2.1: six_agents now runs WITHOUT the graph so full - six_agents cleanly isolates graph gain.
ablations.reuses: Stage 1 + Stage 2 of 'full' run (6 agents + targeted stage 2).

ablations.name: targeted_attacks
ablations.reuses: Stage 1 of 'full' run. Stage 2 runs independently with MAJ_STAGE2_TARGETED=1 (Ozaki-style targeted prompt).
ablations.change_note: In schema v2.0 this config shared stage 2 with direct_judge, producing identical outputs. Fixed in v2.1 by giving it its own stage 2 path.

ablations.name: dung_no_agents
ablations.proposal_label: + Dung Graph (2 agents, zero-shot, with graph)
ablations.note: Minimal agents + zero-shot attack prompt + full graph. Isolates the graph contribution under non-targeted labelling.

ablations.name: full
ablations.note: Canonical full pipeline. The 'full' config writes to the bare <split>/ directory (no suffix) so other configs can reuse it as a shared baseline.

RQ1_graph_contribution.description: full - six_agents: adds graph on top of 6-agent targeted pipeline. This is now the clean graph isolation.

RQ2_agent_diversity.caveat: two_agents uses graph; six_agents does not. The comparison is confounded. A cleaner agent-count ablation would require a 'two_agents_no_graph' config.

RQ3_targeted_vs_zeroshot_attacks.description: targeted_attacks - direct_judge: same inputs, same judge, same no-graph setup. Only stage 2 labelling prompt differs (zero-shot pairwise vs Ozaki targeted).

known_design_caveats: RQ2 comparison (two_agents vs six_agents) is confounded because two_agents still uses graph while six_agents does not. To cleanly isolate agent count, a 9th config 'two_agents_no_graph' would be needed.

The 'direct_judge' config is not a true GPT-4 comparison. It is a local Qwen run using zero-shot pairwise stage 2 relations without graph context. It substitutes for the proposal's GPT-4 Direct Judge row.
dung_no_agents uses zero-shot stage 2, so it is not a clean 'graph only' ablation. It is a '2-agent zero-shot + graph' ablation.
